No Poverty.reason: The course focuses on advanced financial markets and theories, with no explicit or strong implicit connection to poverty eradication or ensuring economic resources for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course covers 'Derivative markets,' which can include food commodity derivatives. This has an indirect, minor relevance to Target 2.c on limiting food price volatility.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content is entirely focused on financial markets and theories, with no connection to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course explicitly aims to equip students with 'problem solving and research skills' and 'professional skills' for employment, directly aligning with Target 4.4 on relevant skills for decent jobs.
evidence: Students will acquire problem solving and research skills.

Gender Equality.reason: Despite a general mention of 'social responsibility,' there is no specific evidence in the syllabus linking the course content to gender equality or related issues.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course content is entirely focused on financial markets and theories, with no connection to clean water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course focuses on general financial markets, without specific mention or emphasis on affordable and clean energy or related investments.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly focuses on financial markets, which are fundamental to achieving economic growth (Target 8.1) and strengthening domestic financial institutions (Target 8.10).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course on financial markets directly relates to Target 9.3 by increasing access to financial services and credit for enterprises, crucial for industrialization and innovation.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: A deep understanding of financial markets, as taught in this course, is essential for improving the regulation and monitoring of global financial markets (Target 10.5), which can help reduce inequalities.
evidence: gains a deep understanding of certain key theory

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course content is entirely focused on financial markets and theories, with no connection to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competency B/G explicitly states students will 'identify the ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability considerations of business situations,' directly supporting Target 12.6 on sustainable practices.
evidence: Students will understand and be able to identify the ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability considerations of business situations.

Climate Action.reason: The course's focus on 'sustainability considerations of business situations' (Core Competency B/G) provides a minimal, indirect link to integrating climate change measures into financial policies (Target 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: The course content is entirely focused on financial markets and theories, with no connection to marine life or ocean conservation.

Life on Land.reason: The course content is entirely focused on financial markets and theories, with no connection to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Understanding financial markets is foundational for addressing illicit financial flows (Target 16.4) and developing effective, accountable, and transparent financial institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The study of financial markets is crucial for enhancing global macroeconomic stability (Target 17.13) and promoting policy coherence for sustainable development (Target 17.14).
